message.content:
Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\CLAUDE.md, then REWRITE it completely with this exact content (replace everything):

```markdown
# MiniMax Agent System — Opus Orchestrated

## Core Architecture

**Opus Max** (claude-opus-4-7-20251120) is the orchestrator.
**MiniMax 2.7** agents do all execution.

Opus holds the full context: stockpile, learnings, agent specs, rules, project state — all simultaneously. MiniMax executes the work. Sonnet is no longer used for orchestration.

## Operating Modes

### Opus Orchestrator Mode
Opus Max receives request → reads _learnings.md + _stockpile.md → pre-reads files → dispatches MiniMax agents → synthesizes results → triggers learning loop

### MiniMax Bystander Mode
MiniMax agents work directly → report to Opus → Opus steps aside for simple one-shots

## Decision Tree

```
Is this a simple task? (single file, one fix)
  → YES → Spawn MiniMax agent directly, report back

Is this multi-step / multi-file / complex?
  → YES → Opus orchestrates: read learnings → pre-read files → dispatch → synthesize → learn

Is this a planning / architecture decision?
  → YES → Opus thinks, may spawn MiniMax for research
```

## Pre-Task Checklist (Opus runs every time)

Before ANY task:
1. Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_learnings.md — apply learnings, never repeat mistakes
2. Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_token_log.md — check budget, warn at 50/75/90%
3. Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\agents\_stockpile.md — know who your agents are
4. Pre-read files ONCE — paste to agents, never let them re-read

## Post-Task Loop (runs after EVERY task automatically)

1. token-budget-agent logs session to _token_log.md
2. learning-agent extracts 3-5 learnings → _learnings.md
3. agent-stockpile-manager benchmarks agents → _stockpile.md

## MiniMax Agent Discipline

**RULE 1: Pre-read once, paste to agents.**
Read each file exactly once. Paste content into agent prompts. No re-reads.

**RULE 2: One agent per file.**
Never spawn two agents on the same file. Group all issues into one agent.

**RULE 3: Group by file, not by issue.**
1 agent × 10 fixes in 1 file > 10 agents × 1 fix each. Target: 2-5 agents per project.

**RULE 4: Opus orchestrates. MiniMax executes.**
Research, synthesis, planning = Opus. Code, fixes, reviews = MiniMax agents.

**RULE 5: Log every session.**
_token_log.md + _learnings.md + _stockpile.md all updated after every task.

## Skill Load Order

For any coding/design/planning task:
1. _learnings.md (apply past learnings)
2. _token_log.md (check budget)
3. _stockpile.md (know active agents)
4. minimax-dev-workflow (dispatch workflow)
5. skill-builder (if building new skills)

## Specialist Agents

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| code-fix-agent | Fix all issues in one file |
| code-review-agent | Rubric-scored review (CRITICAL → LOW) |
| learning-agent | Session retrospectives → _learnings.md |
| token-budget-agent | Budget tracking → _token_log.md |
| git-commit-agent | Conventional commits → push to GitHub |
| agent-stockpile-manager | Benchmark + optimize stockpile → _stockpile.md |

## Token Budget

- Daily limit: 15,000 tokens / 5 hours
- Target: 50-90% per session
- Warn at 50%, 75%, 90%
- See: C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\token-budget-controller.md

## Meta-System

Opus is learning. Every session makes the next one better:
- _learnings.md grows (mistakes + techniques)
- _stockpile.md quality-rates agents
- _token_log.md tracks burn rate

## End Every Response With

**Next:** [1-3 relevant skills/agents]. Skip if purely administrative.
```

content.content:
1	# MiniMax Delegation System
2	
3	## Core Rule
4	
5	Sonnet 4.6 is the orchestrator. MiniMax 2.7 agents do all execution.
6	Sonnet stays in advisory/synthesis mode unless the user requests direct interaction.
7	
8	## Operating Modes
9	
10	### Orchestrator Mode
11	Sonnet receives request → decomposes → spawns MiniMax agents → synthesizes results
12	
13	### Bystander Mode
14	MiniMax agents work directly → report to user → Sonnet steps aside
15	
16	## Decision Tree
17	
18	```
19	Is this a simple task? (single file edit, Q&A, one-shot)
20	  → YES → Spawn MiniMax agent, let it report directly
21	  → NO  → Is this multi-step / multi-file / requires planning?
22	    → YES → Orchestrator mode: Sonnet decomposes, spawns agents, synthesizes
23	    → NO  → Spawn MiniMax agent, review output before showing user
24	```
25	
26	## Spawn Patterns
27	
28	| Pattern | When |
29	|---------|------|
30	| SINGLE | One task → one MiniMax agent |
31	| PARALLEL | Multiple independent sub-tasks → multiple agents simultaneously |
32	| SEQUENTIAL | Chained tasks → output of one feeds into next |
33	| HYBRID | Parallel → synthesize → more parallel → synthesize |
34	
35	**Always ask: "Can I split this into independent pieces and run them simultaneously?" If yes → spawn parallel agents.**
36	
37	## Error Handling
38	
39	```
40	MiniMax agent fails:
41	  → Retry once with same instructions
42	  → Still fails → Sonnet assesses: escalate to user or try different approach
43	  → Partial failure in parallel → collect what worked, report what failed
44	```
45	
46	## Skills Integration
47	
48	When a task matches a known skill (code-review, debugging, etc.):
49	1. Load the relevant skill
50	2. Spawn MiniMax agent with the skill's rules/goals
51	3. MiniMax agent executes using the skill's guidance
52	4. Sonnet reviews/synthesizes the result
53	
54	## Verbosity Control
55	
56	If parallel agent output feels overwhelming, synthesize more aggressively before presenting.
57	
58	## Model Note
59	
60	MiniMax is the default execution model (injected via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + ANTHROPIC_MODEL env vars).
61	All Agent tool calls default to MiniMax automatically.
62	
63	## End Every Response With Skill Suggestions
64	
65	After EVERY response, end with: **Next:** [1-3 relevant skills/agents]. Skip if purely administrative.
66	
67	## Self-Improvement System
68	
69	### Before Every Task
70	1. Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_learnings.md — apply relevant learnings
71	2. Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_token_log.md — check burn rate and budget remaining
72	3. Never repeat the same mistake recorded in _learnings.md
73	
74	### After Every Task
75	1. Log session metrics (tokens, duration, issues fixed) to _token_log.md
76	2. Log session summary to _learnings.md
77	3. Spawn MiniMax agent to extract 3-5 new learnings and append to _learnings.md
78	
79	### Token Budget
80	- Daily limit: 15,000 tokens per 5-hour window
81	- Target: 50-90% per session (7,500–13,500 tokens)
82	- Warn at 50%, 75%, 90% thresholds
83	- See: C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\token-budget-controller.md
84	
85	## MiniMax Agent Discipline (CRITICAL)
86	
87	RULE 1: Never re-read a file you've already read this session.
88	  → Pre-read once, paste content into agent prompts.
89	
90	RULE 2: Never spawn more than one agent on the same file.
91	  → Group all issues for a file into ONE agent.
92	
93	RULE 3: Always group fixes by file, not by issue.
94	  → 1 agent handling 10 issues in 1 file > 10 agents handling 1 issue each.
95	
96	RULE 4: Sonnet explores. MiniMax executes. Never the other way around.
97	  → File reads, research, synthesis = Sonnet.
98	  → Code writing, fixes, refactors = MiniMax agents.
99	
100	RULE 5: Log everything.
101	  → Every session goes to _learnings.md and _token_log.md.
102	  → No session is untracked.
103	
104	## Skill Load Order
105	
106	For any coding/design/planning task:
107	1. minimax-dev-workflow (if dev task)
108	2. token-budget-controller (check budget)
109	3. _learnings.md (apply past learnings)
110	4. minimax-delegation (spawn agents)
111	5. skill-builder (if creating new skills)
112	
113	## Metaswarm Integration
114	
115	Available metaswarm skills (use for complex multi-phase work):
116	- metaswarm:orchestrated-execution — 4-phase loop (IMPLEMENT → VALIDATE → ADVERSARIAL REVIEW → COMMIT)
117	- metaswarm:plan-review-gate — 3 independent reviewers, all must PASS
118	- metaswarm:design-review-gate — 5-agent parallel review
119	- metaswarm:pr-shepherd — monitor PR through to merge
120	- metaswarm:start — begin tracked development work
121	
122	Use these when the task warrants it. For simple fixes, use minimax-dev-workflow directly.
123
